events.title: AI for Visual Disabilities
events.description: Curious about Artificial Intelligence (AI) and what it means for everyday life? AI can help those experiencing loss of vision to experience enhanced independence with everyday life and tasks such as reading text, awareness of objects and surroundings, remembering things, and writing messages. It is also being used in many positive ways around the world — supporting healthcare, education, transportation, and creative work. This four-part workshop series offers a friendly, hands-on introduction to

events.title: LEGO® Club

events.title: Messy Art: Papier Mâché Masks

events.description: Do you want to learn English? Do you want to practice speaking and understanding English and improve your communications skills? events.title: Walk-in Hours with Healthcare Alternative Systems (HAS)
events.display_title: [FREE] Walk-in Hours with Healthcare Alternative Systems (HAS)
events.description: This winter, Healthcare Alternative Systems’ (HAS) Harm Reduction team will be at the Humboldt Park Branch every Tuesday from 1:30pm to 3:30pm. The team focuses on community outreach, connecting vulnerable populations to necessary resources and services. Staff will be providing essential supplies such as warm winter gear, hygiene items, wound care kits, and harm reduction supplies. Whether you have a backyard plot or a sunny balcony, you’ll discover how to make the most of your space. For absolute beginners or any curious gardeners. Each participant will take home a small pot with seeds to kickstart their gardening journey! While here, be sure to visit our Budlong Woods Seed Library.

events.description: Curious about Artificial Intelligence (AI) and what it means for everyday life?
